<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But silence does play a role in justice and in peace.<|8.44|>
<|8.44|> Silence unites us with our spiritual ancestors and with Jesus.<|15.34|>
<|15.34|> And silence actually unites us with all human beings everywhere.<|22.50|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Silence unites people across all kinds of differences.<|6.38|>
<|6.38|> For everyone, every person who's ever been and presumably ever will be needs some measure<|13.28|>
<|13.28|> of silence in order to be human.<|16.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> You need some measure of silence in order to be able to breathe deeply.<|7.96|>
<|7.96|> You need some measure of silence in order to learn anything, if not because you just<|14.12|>
<|14.12|> got to be quiet for a few moments to encounter something new, something you can't comprehend.<|20.96|>
<|20.96|> You've got to be silent in order to eat because you're not supposed to eat with your mouth<|25.88|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We need silence in order to sleep.<|4.78|>
<|5.06|> In summary, we need silence in order to be fully human.<|11.12|>
<|12.50|> May occasional moments of silence then unite us with Christ and with the divine<|19.46|>
<|19.46|> and help us better relate to our neighbors near and far.<|24.86|>
